image_prompt: Four waveform panels A-D in one row, each a sine wave on a fine-gridded box with the x-axis crossing at zero. All four share the SAME amplitude but have different periods: A shortest wavelength (~5 cycles), B ~3 cycles, C ~2 cycles, D longest wavelength (one cycle across the window). Answer = D, because a receding source stretches the wavelength (red-shift / Doppler effect).

render_data.title: Light wave from a stationary source vs a receding source

options.note: Shorter wavelength than stationary — blue-shift (source approaching).
Same wavelength as stationary — no relative motion.
Longest wavelength — wave stretched (red-shift) for a receding source.

render_data.task: Select the graph showing how the wave changes when the source moves away from the observer.

image_prompt: Hubble velocity-distance fan-shaped scatter through the origin. x-axis Distance / Mpc (0, 50, 100), y-axis Velocity / km s⁻¹ (0, 5000, 10⁴). Two populations with a key: grey 'galaxies observed today' forming a wide fan that widens with distance, and teal 'galaxies observed in 1930' clustered near the origin (small distances/velocities) giving the shallow 1930 best-fit and the 1.8 billion year age estimate. Part (d) asks for a best-fit line through the origin.

topic: Resistance vs length of a wire
image_prompt: SVG titled 'A graph of resistance against length for a conducting wire'. x-axis Length / m (0.00 to 1.20 step 0.20), y-axis Resistance / Ω (0.00 to 0.06 step 0.01). Five data points at (0.20,0.015), (0.40,0.027), (0.60,0.033), (0.80,0.045), (1.00,0.056), with a best-fit straight line of gradient ~0.065 Ω/m (variant value) and a NON-ZERO y-intercept of ~0.0075 Ω (evidence of systematic error). Later parts compute the gradient and discuss the intercept.
